title: $display() prints rubbish due to a bug in _vl_vsformat()
body:
---


Author Name: **R. Diez**
Original Redmine Message: 927 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hallo Wilson:

I have upgraded from Ubuntu 12.04 to 12.10, which now comes with GCC 4.7.2, and I'm getting nonsense values when outputting %h and %s arguments with $display() statements. This only happens when compiling the Verilator-generated .cpp files with optimisation level -O3.

After some debugging, I found what the problem is. In file include/verilated.cpp , routine _vl_vsformat(), statement "lwp = qlwp;" is assigning to pointer lwp the address of qlwp[], which immediately goes out of scope, rendering the pointer invalid.

By the way, sometimes I need to run long simulations which generate large amounts of $display() data, and I found it surprising that the $display format string gets parsed every time at runtime. A possible optimisation would be to let Verilator parse the format string upfront and then generate appropriate C++ code like this:

  $display( "blah1 %h blah2 %h", a, b );

  output("blah1 ");
  output_hex(a);
  output(" blah2 ");
  output_hex(b);

One more thing: during the bug hunt, I realised that Verilator itself only gets built with -O, and not with -O2 or -O3.
I tried to modify variables CFLAGS/CXXFLAGS in the top-level configure.ac to no avail, I guess those values are not passed down. In fact, it's proven very hard to pass compilation flags from outside. More worryingly, Verilator is built by default with just "-Wno-unused", and not with "-Wall". The bug I found above would have been a good candidate for a compiler warning (GCC 4.7 doesn't warn though), but no package manager or user would ever realise if the warnings are not turned on. The "don't burden users" comment I found in the sources is a poor excuse (my opinion), and the --enable-ccwarn option for the configure script is not really suitable because of the -Werror flag.

Regards,
  rdiez
